visa_free.notes: EU citizens have the right to free movement and residence under EU Directive 2004/38/EC. No visa or immigration check applies at internal Schengen borders. Unlimited stay rights. Exempt from EES procedure.

eta.nationality: All visa-free Annex II nationals (approx. 61 countries/territories)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be required for all Annex II visa-exempt nationals for Schengen travel. EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026. ETIAS is expected to launch in the last quarter of 2026 (exact date to be announced). After launch, a 6-month transitional period applies before ETIAS becomes mandatory; enforcement is therefore expected no earlier than approximately Q2 2027. Cost approx. EUR 7. Valid for 3 years or until passport expiry, multiple entries, max 90 days per 180-day period. EU/EEA/Schengen and micro-state nationals (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino, Vatican) remain exempt from both EES and ETIAS. IMPORTANT: Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports are subject to a temporary suspension of their visa-free status (effective 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 via EU Commission Implementing Regulation under Art. 8a of Reg. 2018/1806 as amended by Reg. 2025/2441); ordinary Georgian biometric passport holders remain fully visa-free in Annex II.

visa_policy.notes: France applies the common Schengen visa policy as mandated by EU Regulation 2018/1806. Visa-free nationals from Annex II are allowed stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period. EU/EEA/Schengen and micro-state nationals (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino, Vatican) are exempt from both the EES border system and future ETIAS requirement. The EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026 for non-EU/Schengen nationals. ETIAS authorization for visa-free Annex II nationals is expected to launch in late 2026 (exact date to be announced); a 6-month transitional period applies after launch, so mandatory enforcement is expected no earlier than approximately Q2 2027. France has no visa-on-arrival or e-visa schemes for the Schengen zone - visitors either travel visa-free or must obtain a Schengen visa in advance. Vanuatu was permanently removed from Annex II and transferred to Annex I (visa required) via Regulation (EU) 2025/11 effective 3 February 2025. United Kingdom, Hong Kong SAR, and Macao SAR are included in the current consolidated Annex II. Timor-Leste is confirmed visa-free with EU agreement in force since 1 May 2022. Nauru remains in Annex II with a footnote making the exemption conditional on an EU-Nauru visa waiver agreement that has not yet been concluded. Venezuela is confirmed visa-free in Annex II per the December 2025 consolidated version (02018R1806-20251230). The December 2025 amendment (Regulation 2025/2441) restructured the visa suspension mechanism (Articles 8-8f) only; the Annex II country list was unchanged. SUSPENSION ACTIVE: Georgian holders of diplomatic, service or official passports are subject to a temporary suspension of their Schengen visa-free status from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 (EU Commission Implementing Regulation under revised suspension mechanism). Ordinary Georgian biometric passport holders remain fully visa-free under Annex II and are unaffected.

credential.subtype: carte de résident de longue durée-UE (EU long-term residence permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid EU long-term residence card (carte de résident de longue durée-UE) issued by another EU member state (Directive 2003/109/CE), plus eligible family members as defined in Article 2(2) of Directive 2004/38. Exempts holder from requiring a long-stay visa to cross into France. The holder must satisfy general entry conditions (valid travel document, sufficient resources, no SIS alert). Subject to Articles L.313-4-1 and L.313-11-1 of the French Code on Entry and Stay of Foreigners.

credential.subtype: titre de séjour ICT (intra-corporate transferee permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit bearing the mention 'ICT' issued by another EU member state, allowing intra-EU mobility under Directive 2014/66/UE. If the permit is issued by a non-Schengen EU state, the holder must also present a letter from the host company in France specifying the duration of the mobility and the company address. Family members covered under Directive 2003/86/CE Article 4(1). References Articles L.313-7-2 and L.313-24 of the French CESEDA.

cbi.notes: France does not offer a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. There is no direct purchase of French nationality. Citizenship can only be obtained after a minimum of 5 years of lawful regular residence (reduced in some circumstances), meeting language requirements (B2 level French from 1 January 2026), and civic examination. Investor residence through the Talent Passport may eventually lead to naturalization but is not a CBI program.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Economic Investor (Passeport Talent - Investisseur economique)
programs.notes: Requires a direct economic investment of at least EUR 300,000 in tangible or intangible fixed assets. Must create or maintain jobs within four years of investment. Investment may be through personal ownership, a managed company, or a 30%+ capital stake. Pure financial investments excluded. Permit valid up to 4 years, renewable. After 5 years of continuous regular residence, eligible for 10-year permanent residence card permitting any professional activity. Administrative fees: EUR 300 + EUR 50 stamp duty + EUR 99 long-stay visa fee. Confirmed current as of May 2026 via Business France.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Investor
programs.notes: Dedicated fast-track procedure within the Talent Passport framework for foreign investors in the French Tech/startup ecosystem. Requires minimum EUR 300,000 direct investment and commitment to create/preserve jobs within 4 years. Handled by the French Tech Mission on a one-to-one basis. Results in 'Talent - Business Investor' residence permit valid up to 4 years, renewable.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Qualified Employee (Passeport Talent - Salarie qualifie)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals with a Master's degree (or equivalent) and employment contract in France with annual gross salary of at least EUR 39,582 (as of August 2025). Also covers employees of innovative companies (JEI) and intra-company transferees with 3 months seniority.
programs.processing_time: 4 months (implicit refusal if no response within 4 months)
programs.notes: Multi-year residence card valid up to 4 years. No separate work authorization required. Family members (spouse and children) eligible for 'Passeport Talent Famille' permit. Administrative cost: EUR 350 (EUR 300 tax + EUR 50 stamp duty). Applications submitted fully online via administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr.

programs.name: Talent Passport - European Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with at least 3 years of higher education and employment contract with annual gross salary of at least EUR 59,373.
programs.processing_time: 30 days for EU Blue Card holders from other EU states; 4 months for new applicants
programs.notes: EU Blue Card category within the Talent Passport framework. Higher salary threshold than standard qualified employee category. Valid up to 4 years.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Researcher
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a Master's degree plus a hosting agreement with a French research institution. Minimum resources: EUR 2,200/month.
programs.notes: Faster processing (60 days) compared to other Talent Passport categories. Valid for the duration of the hosting agreement up to 4 years.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Founders
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals developing an innovative project in France, recognized as innovative by a public organization (DRIEETS). Minimum financial resources equivalent to minimum wage (SMIC). Must be selected by a partner incubator/accelerator for the expedited French Tech Visa pathway.
programs.processing_time: Not specified; standard Talent Passport timelines apply
programs.notes: Issues 'Talent - Innovative Project' residence permit valid up to 4 years. Family eligible for accompanying status with work rights. After 5 years of regular residence, eligible for permanent residence. From 1 January 2026, B2 French language level required for naturalization.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Employees
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals recruited by innovative startups in France recognized by the Ministry of Economy and Finance. Annual gross salary at least EUR 39,582 (as of August 2025).
programs.processing_time: Not specified; standard Talent Passport timelines apply
programs.notes: Fast-track within the Talent Passport framework specifically for innovative company employees. Issues 'Talent' residence permit valid up to 4 years. Family eligible.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Medical/Pharmacy Professional
programs.eligibility: Non-EU medical or pharmacy professionals with a license to practice in France. Annual salary at least EUR 41,386.48.
programs.notes: Part of the Talent Passport framework. Valid up to 4 years.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Person of International Renown
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with established national or international renown in their field. Minimum resources: EUR 22,404.20.
programs.notes: Part of the Talent Passport framework. Valid up to 4 years. No minimum salary threshold, but proof of reputation required.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Vanuatu removed from visa_free. Regulation (EU) 2025/11 (effective 3 February 2025) transferred Vanuatu from Annex II to Annex I (visa required). The prior record listed Vanuatu as visa-free with an 'unconfirmed post-suspension status' note - it is now confirmed visa-required.

CORRECTION: United Kingdom (GBR) added to visa_free. Post-Brexit UK citizens are listed in Annex II of Regulation 2018/1806-20250203 as confirmed in the current consolidated text. The prior record omitted the UK entirely.
CORRECTION: Hong Kong SAR (HKG) added to visa_free. Hong Kong SAR passport holders are listed in Annex II Part 2 of the consolidated Regulation. The prior record omitted HKG.
CORRECTION: Macao SAR (MAC) added to visa_free. Macao SAR (RAEM) passport holders are listed in Annex II Part 2 of the consolidated Regulation. The prior record omitted MAC.
CORRECTION: Timor-Leste (TLS) added to visa_free. Confirmed in Annex II Part 1; EU-Timor-Leste visa waiver agreement entered into force 1 May 2022. The prior record omitted Timor-Leste.

CORRECTION: Andorra duplicate entries consolidated. The prior record listed Andorra twice (once in a micro-state group with unlimited stay, once in Annex II with 90-day cap). Now consolidated into a single entry reflecting both the Annex II listing and the unlimited-stay/EES-exempt status.
CORRECTION: Monaco, San Marino, Holy See duplicates consolidated similarly. These were listed twice in the prior record.

CORRECTION: Nauru note updated to accurately reflect the Annex II footnote condition: exemption applies only 'from the date of entry into force of an agreement on visa exemption to be concluded with the EU.' No such agreement confirmed concluded as of June 2026.

CORRECTION: ETIAS eta entry updated: (a) count changed from '59 countries/territories' to 'approx. 61 countries/territories' reflecting addition of UK/HKG/MAC/TLS and removal of Vanuatu; (b) source_url updated to the April 2026 EC confirmation page; (c) EES operational status confirmed (10 April 2026).

CORRECTION: USA entry note updated to document the Annexe C exception for US diplomatic/service passport holders travelling for professional reasons (requires visa per Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 Annexe C).
VERIFIED: RBI minimum investment of EUR 300,000 confirmed current as of May 2026 via Business France official page.

VERIFIED: Bilateral diplomatic/service passport exemptions under Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 (Annexe B, in force 19 June 2025) carried forward unchanged - these could not be re-scraped from legifrance.gouv.fr in this validation pass but were confirmed via prior research.

VERIFIED: EU visa waiver agreement in-force dates added to notes for all bilateral bilateral Pacific/Caribbean entries (Dominica, Grenada, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Timor-Leste, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, UAE). Sources: EUR-Lex visa waiver agreements summary.

2026-06-27 CORRECTION: Venezuela (VEN) added to visa_free. Confirmed as Annex II visa-exempt in both the 03.02.2025 and 30.12.2025 consolidated versions of Regulation 2018/1806 (CELEX:02018R1806-20251230). Venezuela was erroneously omitted from all prior versions of this record. Ordinary Venezuelan passport holders are entitled to 90 days visa-free in any 180-day period. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:02018R1806-20251230

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: December 2025 consolidated Regulation 2018/1806 (02018R1806-20251230) re-fetched. Confirms no changes to Annex II country list between February 2025 and December 2025 consolidation; M7 amendment (December 2025) restructured suspension procedures only (Articles 8-8f), not the country listings. All other Annex II entries (Colombia, Antigua and Barbuda, Kiribati, Kosovo, Taiwan, etc.) confirmed present and unchanged.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Colombia (COL) confirmed in Annex II and correctly already listed in visa_free. No change needed.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Annex II source_urls updated to include the December 2025 consolidated text (02018R1806-20251230).

2026-06-29 VERIFIED: All visa_free Annex II entries re-confirmed against December 2025 consolidation (02018R1806-20251230). No additions or removals to the visa-free list between February 2025 and December 2025. Regulation 2025/2441 (published in Official Journal, in force December 2025) reformed the suspension mechanism procedures only; Annex II country list unchanged.

2026-06-29 VERIFIED: Vanuatu confirmed permanently removed from Annex II to Annex I by Regulation (EU) 2025/11 effective 3 February 2025. No reinstatement. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2025/11/oj/eng

2026-06-29 VERIFIED: Venezuela (VEN) confirmed in Annex II as ordinary-passport visa-free per search results from etias.com and visalogy.com. Consistent with December 2025 consolidation. No change needed.

2026-06-29 NOTE: Georgia (GEO) ordinary passport holders remain visa-free in Annex II. However, the EU Commission suspended visa-free travel for Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 (first use of reinforced visa suspension mechanism under Regulation 2025/2441). Source: https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_564 and https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/commission-implementing-regulation-temporary-suspension-visa-exemption-georgian-nationals-holding_en. The ordinary-passport visa_free entry for Georgia is NOT affected. The conditional_access section does not list Georgia for diplomatic passports (since there was previously no bilateral diplomatic exemption listed for Georgia under Annexe B), so no conditional_access entry requires deletion. The suspension means Georgian diplomatic/official passport holders currently require a full Schengen visa.

2026-06-29 CORRECTION: ETIAS eta note updated to clarify that a 6-month transitional period applies after the Q4 2026 launch, meaning mandatory ETIAS enforcement is expected no earlier than approximately Q2 2027. Source: https://schengentraveler.com/etias-launch-date/ and https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/main-differences-between-ees-and-etias-what-travellers-need-know-2026-04-28_en
gaps: Nauru: No EU-Nauru visa waiver agreement found in EUR-Lex summary as of June 2026. Nauru is in Annex II but with an agreement-contingent footnote. Practical visa-free status is legally ambiguous; listed with a conditional note.

Congo (Brazzaville) diplomatic passport waiver: The Annexe B waiver stated expiry of '30 septembre 2024'. Not included in conditional_access pending confirmation of renewal or lapse from legifrance.gouv.fr - legifrance was not scrapeable in this pass.
France-Visas.gouv.fr returned HTTP 403 and could not be scraped directly for the full visa wizard nationality list in this validation pass.

Legifrance.gouv.fr Annexe B/C of the Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 (updated 19 June 2025) was not directly re-scraped in this validation pass. The diplomatic/service passport conditional_access entries are carried forward from prior research; the full list of countries covered by Annexe B should be re-verified against the 19 June 2025 version when legifrance becomes accessible.

ETIAS precise launch date and definitive count of eligible nationalities: official sources as of June 2026 confirm 'last quarter of 2026' but no specific date. Mandatory enforcement expected approximately Q2 2027 after 6-month transitional period. Nationality count listed as 'approx. 61' pending official enumeration.
France's overseas territories (French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis and Futuna, etc.) may have different visa rules; only metropolitan France / standard Schengen rules captured here.
Tonga EU visa waiver agreement status: listed as 'provisional application' since 2015. Formal entry into force date not confirmed from official sources in this pass.
UAE (ARE) EU visa waiver agreement: listed as 'provisional application' from 6 May 2015. Formal entry into force date not confirmed from official sources in this pass.

Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport suspension (6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027): The conditional_access section does not list Georgia for diplomatic passports since no bilateral Annexe B exemption existed. The suspension information is captured in validation_notes and visa_policy.notes but not as a separate conditional_access entry (since the baseline condition is visa-required, not visa-free-becoming-restricted). Confirm end-date renewal/extension after 6 March 2027.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Tourism / Private Visit
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals not exempt from Schengen visa requirements to visit France and the Schengen Area for tourism, private visits, or family stays for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Fee is EUR 80 for adults, EUR 40 for children aged 6-11, free for children under 6. Applied for at the French consulate or VFS/TLS Contact centre in applicant's country of residence. Schengen-wide visa; allows travel to all Schengen states. An attestation d'accueil (host certificate, cost EUR 30) is required for private/family visits. Processing begins no earlier than 3 months before departure. Single-entry variants also exist.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Required for nationals of certain countries to transit through the international zone of a French airport without entering French territory, between two legs of an international journey.
visa_types.notes: Fee is EUR 80. Only required for nationals of countries listed in the common EU airport transit visa list (e.g. Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Congo DR, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Ghana, India, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Turkey). Holders of valid Schengen, US, Canadian, or UK visas/residence permits are often exempt. Applied for at the French consulate.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Business
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals requiring a Schengen visa to travel to France for business meetings, conferences, trade fairs, or commercial activities for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Same Type C Schengen visa as tourist; purpose is distinguished by supporting documentation (e.g. invitation letter from French company, registration at trade fair). Fee EUR 80. Multiple-entry visas for frequent business travellers may be issued for up to 5 years. Applicants must not carry out paid work in France under this visa.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Student Visa (VLS-TS Etudiant)
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU nationals to reside in France for the duration of their studies (above 3 months) at a French higher education institution or language school. Serves as both a visa and residence permit (VLS-TS) for the first year.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). For higher education: application must be made via the Campus France / Etudes en France procedure in applicable countries. VLS-TS Student visa validates within 3 months of arrival via ANEF online platform. Fee variable (EUR 99 standard long-stay fee; some nationalities exempt including scholarship holders). Must be validated within 3 months of arrival. After first year, apply for student residence card at prefecture. Part-time work (up to 964 hours/year) is permitted.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Minor Student Visa (VLS-TS Etudiant Mineur)
visa_types.purpose: Authorises foreign minors under 18 to attend a French school or educational programme lasting more than 3 months, while their parents reside abroad.
visa_types.notes: Maximum duration 11 months. Must not require a separate residence card (no prefecture registration required). Applicable to minors attending school in France whose parents live abroad. Applied for at the French consulate in the country of residence.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Work Visa - Salaried Employee (Salarié / Travailleur Temporaire)
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-EU nationals to work in France under an employment contract, either as a permanent employee (salarié) or temporary worker (travailleur temporaire).
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). Employer must obtain work authorisation from DREETS (regional labour authority) before the visa can be issued, unless the occupation is on the shortage list. Visa fee EUR 99; some nationalities exempt (seasonal workers, etc.). After arrival, must apply for salarié or travailleur temporaire residence card at prefecture. Salarié card valid up to 1 year, renewable. Occupation-in-tension list allows simplified procedure.

visa_types.name: Talent Passport Visa (Passeport Talent - multiple subcategories)
visa_types.purpose: Multi-year long-stay visa for highly skilled workers, researchers, senior executives, artists of international renown, innovative company founders, and investors. The Passeport Talent consolidates multiple former permit types into a single framework.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 4 years. Subcategories include: Salarié qualifié (qualified employee, min salary EUR 39,582/yr + Master's or equivalent); EU Blue Card (min salary EUR 59,373/yr + 3 years higher education); Chercheur (researcher, hosting agreement + min EUR 2,200/month); Projet innovant (startup founder); Investisseur économique (EUR 300,000+ investment + job creation); Professions médicales/pharmaceutiques; Personne de renommée internationale. No separate work permit required. Administrative fee EUR 350 (EUR 300 tax + EUR 50 stamp duty). Online application via ANEF. Family members eligible for Passeport Talent Famille permit.

visa_types.name: ICT (Intra-Company Transferee) Visa (Salarié détaché ICT)
visa_types.purpose: Allows managers, specialists, or trainees employed by a multinational company to be transferred to the company's French affiliate, subsidiary, or branch for a temporary assignment.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D), implemented under EU Directive 2014/66/EU. Maximum 3 years for managers/specialists, 1 year for trainees. Requires minimum 3 months seniority with the sending company. Salary must equal at least what a French employee in equivalent position earns. Intra-EU mobility: holders of ICT permit from another EU state may move to France for up to 90 days without a new visa.

visa_types.name: Seasonal Worker Visa (Travailleur Saisonnier)
visa_types.purpose: Authorises non-EU nationals to undertake seasonal agricultural or tourist-sector work in France for a maximum of 6 months per year without requiring a full residence permit.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D) with multiple-entry for repeated seasonal stays. Maximum 6 months in any 12-month period. Visa fee may be waived for seasonal workers. Employer must have a seasonal work contract pre-approved by DREETS. Card may be renewed annually for recurring seasonal workers.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa (Visa Vacances-Travail / Programme Vacances-Travail)
visa_types.purpose: Enables young adults from partner countries to stay in France for up to 12 months (or 24 months for certain nationalities) for leisure and incidental work, to supplement their travel funds.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). Available under bilateral agreements with: Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Canada (up to 24 months), Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Hong Kong SAR, Japan, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Russia, South Korea, Taiwan, Uruguay. Age limits typically 18-30 or 18-35 depending on the bilateral agreement. Applicant must have sufficient funds (proof of at least EUR 2,500 typically required), valid health insurance, and no dependent children accompanying. Work must be secondary to travel. No residence card required on arrival for most nationalities.

visa_types.name: Family Reunification Long-Stay Visa (Regroupement Familial)
visa_types.purpose: Enables non-EU family members (spouse, minor children) of foreign nationals legally residing in France to join them through the official family reunification procedure.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). The sponsoring family member in France must have been legally resident for at least 18 months, have adequate housing and stable income. Application submitted to OFII (French Office of Immigration and Integration). After arrival, family members must apply for a 'Vie privée et familiale' residence card at the prefecture within 2 months. Sponsor must also demonstrate receipt of OFII medical examination.

visa_types.name: Spouse of French National Visa (Conjoint de Français)
visa_types.purpose: Allows a non-EU national who is the legally recognised spouse of a French citizen to enter and reside in France, with the visa serving as a temporary residence permit.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay VLS-TS visa (Type D) valid 1 year, exempted from visa fee. Must be validated through ANEF within 3 months of arrival. After 1 year the holder may obtain a multi-year residence card. Marriage must be legally recognised. PACS (civil partnership) partners of French nationals may also be eligible for a long-stay visa but through the general family category.

visa_types.name: Visitor Long-Stay Visa (Visa Visiteur / Retraité)
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU nationals wishing to reside in France without working - including retirees and persons of independent means - to stay long-term provided they have sufficient financial resources and health insurance.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). Applicant must prove sufficient stable income or savings to support themselves without working (typically at least EUR 1,200-1,500/month recommended by consulates). Private health insurance covering France is required for the first year. After arrival must apply for 'Visiteur' residence card at prefecture. Work of any kind is strictly prohibited under this status. Temporary long-stay variant available for 4-6 month stays without need for prefecture registration.

visa_types.name: Temporary Long-Stay Visa - Short Studies / Cultural Activities
visa_types.purpose: Covers stays of 4 to 6 months for short educational programmes (language courses, professional training), artistic activities, or visits by persons with sufficient independent means who do not need a full long-stay permit.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D) valid 4-6 months. No prefecture registration or residence card required. The holder cannot extend this visa or convert it to a residence card while in France; a new application must be made from abroad if a longer stay is needed. Suitable for courses under 6 months, internships of limited duration, and other temporary activities.

visa_types.name: Talent Passport - Economic Investor Visa (Passeport Talent Investisseur Economique)
visa_types.purpose: Grants residency rights to non-EU nationals making a direct economic investment of at least EUR 300,000 in tangible or intangible fixed assets in a French company, with a commitment to create or maintain employment.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa under the Talent Passport framework. Minimum EUR 300,000 direct investment in tangible or intangible fixed assets. Must create or maintain jobs within 4 years. Pure financial investments excluded. Administrative fee EUR 349 (EUR 300 tax + EUR 50 stamp duty - EUR 1 discount via online). After 5 years of continuous regular residence eligible for 10-year permanent residence card. French Tech Visa fast-track variant available for investors in the startup ecosystem.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign diplomatic personnel, government officials, and their dependants for official missions and postings in France. Also covers diplomatic passport holders from countries with bilateral exemption agreements for short stays.
visa_types.notes: Fee is waived for diplomatic and official visas. Issued by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs diplomatic missions. Duration corresponds to the duration of the official posting. Many diplomatic and service passport holders from countries listed in Annexe B of the Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 (as amended, in force 19 June 2025) are exempt from any visa requirement for stays up to 3 months.

visa_types.name: Au Pair Visa (Jeune au pair)
visa_types.purpose: Allows young foreigners to live with a French host family, participate in family life, and perform light childcare duties, while attending French language classes.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). Governed by the Strasbourg Agreement on au pair placement (1969). Requires a formal au pair placement contract (agreement) approved by the French labour authorities. Applicant must be aged 18-30, have adequate knowledge of French, and be enrolled in or commit to French language courses. Host family provides board, lodging, and pocket money. Duration usually up to 1 year, renewable once.

visa_types.name: Intern / Trainee Visa (Stagiaire / Stagiaire ICT)
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-EU nationals to undertake professional internships (conventions de stage) or intracompany training assignments in France for periods exceeding 3 months.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay visa (Type D). The trainee/intern category requires a signed training convention (agreement). The ICT (Intracompany Transferee) Trainee subtype is for trainees within multinationals, governed by EU Directive 2014/66/EU, maximum 1 year. Standard intern visa (stagiaire) is for academic internships. Minimum training allowance applies per French labour law.

outbound_official.note: No single official French government outbound visa-list page located analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The French Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Ministère de l'Europe et des Affaires étrangères) publishes 'Conseils aux voyageurs' (Travel Advice) at https://www.diplomatie.gouv.fr/fr/conseils-aux-voyageurs as a per-country lookup tool, where each destination country page includes an 'Entrée / séjour' section describing entry requirements for French citizens. There is no consolidated page that groups all destinations by visa category (visa-free, visa on arrival, e-visa, ETA). The service-public.gouv.fr page https://www.service-public.gouv.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F32833 only directs French citizens to consult individual destination pages. Rely on destination-side data and the per-country pages on diplomatie.gouv.fr for outbound requirements.
